definition: Combined $2.7-3.3T annual private-sector Treasury absorption (QT runoff ~$720B + net new issuance ~$2.0-2.6T) is 5-6x the QE-era level, adding an estimated 50-100bp to corporate cost of capital above what the policy rate implies. IG corporate yield (5.3-5.8%) exceeds standard models of default probability plus risk-free rate by this amount.
evidence: IG corporate yield 50-100bp above standard model predictions
open_questions: How much of the 50-100bp residual is crowding-out vs global risk aversion or dollar liquidity premium?
Is the premium sensitive to QT pace changes?
Does private credit absorb some of this pressure?
